searchLedger.hypothesis: Changed code comments do not contain banned ephemeral labels

title: No regression test combines specFolder filtering with deprecated-tier filtering
evidence: The scoped fill test uses specFolder with only normal target rows at lines 517-538, while the deprecated fill test omits specFolder at lines 541-563. There is no test where higher-ranked rows include both out-of-scope and deprecated candidates while the call also passes specFolder.
rationale: The two core old-code regressions are covered, but a future regression that applies deprecated filtering only for unscoped searches, or scope filtering without deprecated exclusion, could pass this suite. The exact survivor-count boundary is covered by limit 3 with exactly 3 valid survivors; this gap is specifically the combined-filter case.

title: Metadata-filtered in-memory BM25 can build a full-corpus candidate and SQL parameter list
evidence: candidateLimit becomes index.getStats().documentCount whenever specFolder || db at lines 428-433, then all returned ids are bound into one memory_index IN (...) lookup at lines 452-456. bm25-index.ts sorts then slices results, so the new overhead is returning/binding all matching ids for metadata filtering.
rationale: This is bounded by corpus size and bypassed when the SQLite lexical engine is selected, so it is not a proven P0 latency blow-up. It can still regress broad/common-term fallback searches on large in-memory corpora and may approach SQLite parameter limits. Consider iterative over-fetch or chunked metadata resolution instead of one full-corpus metadata query.
